items.headline: Uber and Lucid team up to bring robotaxis to Houston
items.summary: Yahoo Finance Senior Autos Reporter Pras Subramanian joins the show to discuss why Uber (UBER) and Lucid (LCID) chose Houston, Texas, as their next robotaxi market, what makes the city an attractive testing ground for autonomous ride-hailing and why there's so much riding on this launch for both companies.
items.why_it_matters: News impact is unclear; monitor price and volume reaction.

items.headline: Musk Swaps 300 Million Tesla Options for Shares He Can Vote
items.summary: Elon Musk boosted his voting power in Tesla to 20% by turning some of his stock options into shares that he can immediately vote but can’t sell until 2028.  Fresh off last week’s SpaceX IPO, Musk made good on an April agreement with Tesla to convert outstanding options on roughly 300 million shares that he received from a 2018 pay package, securities filings show.  The unusual transaction meant Musk didn’t walk away with more plain-vanilla Tesla shares.
items.why_it_matters: News impact is unclear; monitor price and volume reaction.

items.headline: Qualcomm eyes $10 billion AI shortcut as smartphone growth slows
items.summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) helped define the smartphone era. Now the chip giant is striving to prove it can play a far bigger role in the artificial intelligence boom. That may take a lot. items.headline: Sequoia’s Botha Joins SpaceX Board
items.summary: Sequoia Capital’s Roelof Botha, who previously served as the firm’s top leader, has joined SpaceX as a director on its board. Botha will serve as an independent director, expanding the new public company’s board to nine people including Chief Executive Elon Musk and President Gwynne Shotwell.
items.why_it_matters: News may increase risk, valuation pressure, or negative sentiment.
